'三重県松阪市櫛田町724', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:櫛田神社 (松阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544726}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu||WD=|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

'三重県松阪市櫛田町724', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:櫛田神社 (松阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544726}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

'三重県松阪市櫛田町724', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:櫛田神社 (松阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544726}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247|1=January 11}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247|1=January 11}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247|1=January 11}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県松阪市櫛田町724', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:櫛田神社 (松阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544726}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kushida Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247|1=January 11}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:松阪市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:松阪市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

画像=[[Image:Matsusaka_kushida_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}} Kushida-chō 724 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E|display=title,inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}} and others | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) or earlier | 本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|WD=Q2247|1=January 11}} | 地図={{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=32|lat_sec=58|lon_deg=136|lon_min=35|lon_sec=21|label=Kushida Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|58|N|136|35|21|E}}}} }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kushida-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsusaka|Q460829|WD=Q460829|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsusaka, Mie]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsusaka, Mie]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is located near the west bank of the lower {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}}. It is revered by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=barber|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=beauty|Q2474068|WD=Q2474068|1=Cosmetology}} professionals, and near the precinct stands a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” built by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. {{ill|lt=Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|Q865839|WD=Q865839|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida Shrine}} in Hakata Ward, Fukuoka City, known for July’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Q1749262|WD=Q1749262|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} and October’s {{ill|lt=Hakata Okunchi|Q11408922|WD=Q11408922|1=Hakata Okunchi}}, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=757|Q31135|WD=Q31135|1=AD 757}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1) by inviting this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ōwakago-no-Mikoto|Q111114032|WD=Q111114032|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto}}, Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, according to ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}'', was a powerful local ruler governing “Takeda no Kuni”; after {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} interacted with him while visiting this area, a comb fell, so the place was named Kushida and “Kushida-sha” was established[https://www.matsusaka-kanko.com/information/2017/01/06/櫛田神社/] - “Kushida Shrine,” Matsusaka Tourism Association ''{{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|Q131331326|WD=Q131331326|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}''. Also, according to the shrine history, because Ōwakago-no-Mikoto achieved many military merits, Yamatohime-no-Mikoto ordered that he be enshrined as this shrine’s deity. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 22 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} ({{ill|lt=8 BCE|Q28200|WD=Q28200|1=Ante Christum natum}}), when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was searching for a place to enshrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and enshrined Ōwakago-no-Mikoto, who guided her here. Around the {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} period in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, shrine buildings were lost and worship lapsed, but it was revived in the {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} era. On {{ill|lt=February 27|Q2361|WD=Q2361|1=February 27}}, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was relocated to Aza Murogaito and renamed Ōkushida Shrine with {{ill|lt=merger|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}; on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was merged into {{ill|lt=Kamiyama Shrine|Q17988632|WD=Q17988632|1=Iinotakamiya-kouyama Shrine}} in Yamazoe-chō. Later, {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} requested a {{ill|lt=bunshi|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}}, and on {{ill|lt=February 1|Q2312|WD=Q2312|1=February 1}}, {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 8), a divided shrine was re-established at the current site. On {{ill|lt=September 4|Q2855|WD=Q2855|1=September 4}}, {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|WD=Q2021|1=2006}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 18) (Comb Day), a “{{ill|lt=comb|Q23834|WD=Q23834|1=Comb}} monument” was erected by the Mie Prefectural Beauty and Sanitation Trade Association and others. The calligraphy was by then {{ill|lt=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare|Q841962|WD=Q841962|1=Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare}} {{ill|lt=Jirō Kawasaki|Q1689655|WD=Q1689655|1=Jirō Kawasaki}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kushida River|Q6447936|WD=Q6447936|1=Kushida River}} * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Line|Q900630|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|lt=Kushida Station|Q1710084|WD=Q1710084|1=Kushida Station}} * {{ill|lt=Sana Shrine|Q17226795|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} === External links === * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%AB%9B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 櫛田神社] - Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}-07-30) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsusaka, Mie]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsusaka, Mie]]
